title: Form Attributes
content:
### HTML Form Attributes

The `<form>` element has important attributes that control behavior.

---

### The `action` Attribute

Defines where to send the form data:

```html
<form action="/submit-form.php">
<form action="https://api.example.com/submit">
<form action="">  <!-- Submit to current page -->
```

---

### The `method` Attribute

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `get` | Appends data to URL (visible, for searches) |
| `post` | Sends data in body (hidden, for sensitive data) |

```html
<form method="get">  <!-- URL: ?name=John&age=25 -->
<form method="post"> <!-- Data in request body -->
```

---

### The `target` Attribute

| Value | Where to Open |
|-------|---------------|
| `_self` | Same tab (default) |
| `_blank` | New tab |

```html
<form action="/submit" target="_blank">
```

---

### The `enctype` Attribute

Only needed when a form has file uploads:

| Value | Use |
|-------|-----|
| `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` | Default |
| `multipart/form-data` | When uploading files |
| `text/plain` | Plain text (rare) |

```html
<form action="/upload" method="post"
      enctype="multipart/form-data">
  <input type="file" name="document">
</form>
```

---

### The `autocomplete` Attribute

```html
<form autocomplete="on">  <!-- Browser fills fields -->
<form autocomplete="off">  <!-- No auto-fill -->
```

---

### The `novalidate` Attribute

Turns off browser validation:

```html
<form novalidate>
  <input type="email" required>
  <!-- Will submit even with invalid email -->
</form>
```

> **Best Practice:** Use `post` for forms that change data (registration, login), `get` for search forms where the results can be bookmarked.
codeExample:
<form action="/search" method="get" target="_blank">
  <label for="q">Search:</label>
  <input type="search" id="q" name="q">
  <input type="submit" value="Search">
</form>

<form action="/register" method="post" autocomplete="on">
  <label for="email">Email:</label>
  <input type="email" id="email" name="email" required>
  <input type="submit" value="Register">
</form>

quiz.question: Which method should be used for form data that should NOT appear in the URL?
